title: globularia cordifolia subsp. repens
intro: Globularia cordifolia subsp. repens, also known as creeping globularia, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae, characterized by its heart-shaped leaves and blue flowers that bloom in the summer. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Globularia cordifolia subsp. repens include its potential to protect against oxidative stress, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
